# Copilot Instructions — PL/I Analysis Workspace

This workspace is dedicated to analyzing, documenting, and generating IBM Enterprise PL/I for z/OS code.

---

## Repository Structure

```
pli-workspace/
├── pli_src/                    # Drop PL/I source files here (.pli, .pl1, .txt)
├── docs/
│   ├── programs/               # Per-program documentation (generated by PLI-Documenter)
│   ├── dependencies/           # Cross-program dependency charts (PLI-DependencyMapper)
│   ├── flows/                  # Control-flow diagrams (PLI-FlowDiagram)
│   └── reviews/                # Best-practice review reports (PLI-Reviewer)
├── generated/                  # New PL/I code output (PLI-CodeGen)
├── .github/
│   ├── copilot-instructions.md # This file
│   ├── agents/                 # Agent definitions
│   │   ├── pli-documenter.agent.md
│   │   ├── pli-dependency-mapper.agent.md
│   │   ├── pli-flow-diagram.agent.md
│   │   ├── pli-reviewer.agent.md
│   │   └── pli-codegen.agent.md
│   └── skills/                 # Reusable skill definitions
│       ├── pli-language/SKILL.md
│       ├── documentation/SKILL.md
│       ├── diagrams/SKILL.md
│       ├── review/SKILL.md
│       └── codegen/SKILL.md
└── .vscode/
    └── mcp.json                # MCP server configuration (custom-pli-mcp)
```

---

## MCP Server

The `custom-pli-mcp` server provides access to indexed IBM Enterprise PL/I for z/OS 6.2 documentation:

- **PL/I Language Reference** (Parts 1–4) — syntax, built-in functions, attributes, statements
- **PL/I Programming Guide** (Parts 1–3) — compiler options, I/O, interfacing, optimization
- **PL/I Messages and Codes** — compile-time and runtime messages, error codes, diagnostics
- **PL/I Compiler and Run-Time Migration Guide** — migration from older compilers, changed behaviors, new compiler messages
- **z/OS Language Environment Programming Guide** (Parts 1–3) — LE runtime environment, condition handling, storage management, interlanguage communication

### Available Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `search` | Semantic search across all indexed PDF documents |
| `fetch` | Retrieve full page content by document title and page numbers |

Use the MCP server to look up PL/I language constructs, built-in functions, compiler options, coding patterns, compiler messages, migration guidance, and Language Environment runtime services whenever analyzing or generating code.

---

## Agents

| Agent | Purpose | Output Location |
|-------|---------|-----------------|
| **PLI-Documenter** | Analyze PL/I source and create comprehensive documentation | `docs/programs/` |
| **PLI-DependencyMapper** | Map cross-program dependencies (CALLs, %INCLUDE, shared files) | `docs/dependencies/` |
| **PLI-FlowDiagram** | Generate Mermaid control-flow diagrams | `docs/flows/` |
| **PLI-Reviewer** | Review code against PL/I best practices | `docs/reviews/` |
| **PLI-CodeGen** | Generate new PL/I code for specified scenarios | `generated/` |

### Agent Workflow

1. Place PL/I source files in `pli_src/`
2. Use `@PLI-Documenter` to create documentation for individual programs
3. Use `@PLI-DependencyMapper` to map relationships across programs
4. Use `@PLI-FlowDiagram` to visualize program logic
5. Use `@PLI-Reviewer` to check code against best practices
6. Use `@PLI-CodeGen` to generate new PL/I code

Agents can be chained: Documenter output feeds into DependencyMapper and FlowDiagram. Reviewer output can feed into CodeGen for generating corrected code.

---

## Conventions

- All diagrams use **Mermaid** format (renders natively in GitHub and VS Code)
- Documentation files are Markdown with YAML frontmatter for metadata
- Program names follow the PL/I source member name (e.g., `CRTPLN3`)
- The MCP `search` tool should be used to verify PL/I language constructs before documenting
- The MCP `fetch` tool should be used when deeper language reference detail is needed
- Never fabricate PL/I syntax — always verify against the indexed documentation
